lesson_title: Defensive Play: Stiff Hands vs Weak Dealer

introduction.text: Welcome to the hardest part of Blackjack. You have a 'Stiff Hand' (12 to 16). If you hit, you might bust. If you stand, your score is low. The secret? Look at ME. If I have a weak card (2, 3, 4, 5, 6), you don't need to beat me. You just need to let me beat myself.

scenarios.title: The Classic Standoff (16 vs 6)
scenarios.goal: Teach standing on 16 against a weak 6.

sequence.text: You have 16. It's a terrible hand. But look at my 6. That is a 'Bust Card'. I will likely have to take cards and go over 21. Don't risk busting yourself. Just STAND.

finish.text: Exactly! I had 15, had to hit, and caught a 10. I busted. You won with a weak 16 just by doing nothing.

scenarios.title: The Nervous 13 (13 vs 3)
scenarios.goal: Reinforce standing on 13+ against weak cards.

sequence.text: You have 13. My 3 is weak, but tricky. Still, the math says: if you hit 13, you will likely bust. Let me take the risk. Click STAND.

finish.text: Phew! I had 13, drew a King, and busted. Playing defensively pays off.

scenarios.title: The Exception (12 vs 2)
scenarios.goal: Teach that 12 vs 2 requires a HIT.

sequence.text: You have 12. Dealer has 2. BE CAREFUL! 12 is a special case. Dealer's 2 is not weak enough to guarantee a bust. We must take one card. Hit!
You got an 8! Total 20. Now you are safe. Stand.

finish.text: Good call. I ended up with 17. Your 12 would have lost, but your 20 wins.

scenarios.title: The Trap (12 vs 4)
scenarios.goal: Contrast with previous scenario: 12 vs 4 is a Stand.

sequence.text: Again 12. But now I have a 4. A 4 is much weaker than a 2 for the dealer. Now the strategy changes back to defense. Do not hit. STAND.

finish.text: Correct. I had 14, hit an 8, and busted. You survived with just 12 points.

scenarios.title: Bust Magnet (15 vs 5)
scenarios.goal: Handling the worst hand vs a weak dealer.

sequence.text: You have 15. This is statistically one of the worst hands. Hitting is suicide. I have a 5. I am in trouble too. Don't intervene. Let me destroy my own hand. STAND.

finish.text: Dealer Bust! I had 14 and drew a King. Remember: When the dealer is weak, patience is your weapon.

conclusion.text: You learned how to defend against a weak dealer. But what if I have a STRONG card (7, 8, 9, 10, Ace) and you have a weak hand? That is a nightmare scenario. We will face it in the next lesson!
